channel.title: Feeds Student Journalism and feeds about Journalism feeds

item.title: UC basic needs programs might be in danger. What now?
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO – Nearly half of all UC undergraduates and one-third of graduate students are currently facing food insecurity, according to data presented at a UC Board of Regents meeting Wednesday.
item.source:markdown: SAN FRANCISCO – Nearly half of all UC undergraduates and one-third of graduate students are currently facing food insecurity, according to data presented at a UC Board of Regents meeting Wednesday.

item.title: UCLA softball to test win streak against Rutgers in first road conference series
item.description: Momentum is defined in physics as the product of mass and velocity. But for UCLA softball, it looks like a 19-game win streak. No. 7 UCLA softball (25-3, 6-0 Big Ten) will take on another Big Ten opponent in Rutgers (16-12, 3-3), at the Rutgers Softball Complex in Piscataway from March 21 to March 23.
item.source:markdown: Momentum is defined in physics as the product of mass and velocity. But for UCLA softball, it looks like a 19-game win streak. No. 7 UCLA softball (25-3, 6-0 Big Ten) will take on another Big Ten opponent in Rutgers (16-12, 3-3), at the Rutgers Softball Complex in Piscataway from March 21 to March 23.

item.title: Committee recommends removal of Chávez’s name from Chicana/o studies department
item.description: UCLA’s Honorific Naming Committee unanimously recommended the removal of Cesár E. Chávez’s name from the department of Chicana/o and Central American Studies on Friday, following a Wednesday New York Times investigation which revealed his alleged sex abuse.  The committee – which received the request from the department Wednesday – sent its recommendation to Chancellor Julio Frenk and Executive Vice Chancellor and Provost Darnell Hunt, according to a Friday letter obtained by the Daily Bruin.
item.source:markdown: UCLA’s Honorific Naming Committee unanimously recommended the removal of Cesár E. Chávez’s name from the department of Chicana/o and Central American Studies on Friday, following a Wednesday New York Times investigation which revealed his alleged sex abuse. The committee – which received the request from the department Wednesday – sent its recommendation to Chancellor Julio Frenk and Executive Vice Chancellor and Provost Darnell Hunt, according to a Friday letter obtained by the Daily Bruin.

item.title: United Auto Workers units finalize agreements with UC, avoid strike
item.description: Three United Auto Workers units ratified contracts with the UC on Friday, averting a strike after months of bargaining.  The three units – UAW Local 4811’s academic student employees unit, Research and Public Service Professionals-UAW and Student Services and Advising Professionals-UAW – represent more than 40,000 UC academic and research employees, including teaching assistants.
item.source:markdown: Three United Auto Workers units ratified contracts with the UC on Friday, averting a strike after months of bargaining. The three units – UAW Local 4811’s academic student employees unit, Research and Public Service Professionals-UAW and Student Services and Advising Professionals-UAW – represent more than 40,000 UC academic and research employees, including teaching assistants.

item.title: Q&A: Joachim Trier reflects on politics, family and cinema after Oscars win
item.description: This post was updated March 20 at 9:50 p.m. “Sentimental Value” has found its place in the Oscars record books. The Norwegian psychological drama from director Joachim Trier earned the Academy Award for Best International Feature Film at Sunday’s 98th annual Oscars ceremony.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated March 20 at 9:50 p.m._ “Sentimental Value” has found its place in the Oscars record books. The Norwegian psychological drama from director Joachim Trier earned the Academy Award for Best International Feature Film at Sunday’s 98th annual Oscars ceremony.

item.title: Gymnastics looks to carry home-crowd momentum into Big Ten championship
item.description: The lights have dimmed in Pauley Pavilion. But the Bruins will not leave their home crowd magic in Westwood. “We bring Pauley everywhere,” said senior Jordan Chiles.
item.source:markdown: The lights have dimmed in Pauley Pavilion. But the Bruins will not leave their home crowd magic in Westwood. “We bring Pauley everywhere,” said senior Jordan Chiles.

item.title: UCPD investigates e-scooter stolen from Dodd Hall amid rise in thefts
item.description: UCPD is investigating an electric scooter theft that happened at Dodd Hall on Wednesday evening, according to a Friday Clery Timely Warning. Multiple e-scooters have been stolen across campus, with recent incidents occurring near Dodd Hall and Lu Valle Commons, according to the alert.
item.source:markdown: UCPD is investigating an electric scooter theft that happened at Dodd Hall on Wednesday evening, according to a Friday Clery Timely Warning. Multiple e-scooters have been stolen across campus, with recent incidents occurring near Dodd Hall and Lu Valle Commons, according to the alert.

item.title: Attending UCLA’s School of Dentistry is going to get more expensive
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO – The UC Board of Regents approved a tuition increase for UCLA’s dentistry program at its Wednesday meeting. The academic and student affairs committee approved the tuition increase for the dentistry program – as well as for a nursing program at UC Irvine and pharmacy programs at UCI and UC San Francisco – at its bimonthly meeting, held Tuesday and Wednesday at UCSF.
item.source:markdown: SAN FRANCISCO – The UC Board of Regents approved a tuition increase for UCLA’s dentistry program at its Wednesday meeting. The academic and student affairs committee approved the tuition increase for the dentistry program – as well as for a nursing program at UC Irvine and pharmacy programs at UCI and UC San Francisco – at its bimonthly meeting, held Tuesday and Wednesday at UCSF.

item.title: UCLA men’s tennis looks to extend win streak in Wisconsin, Nebraska matchups
item.description: Rivalries bring intensity. But once the matchups are in the rearview, teams look to carry that energy forward. No. 24 UCLA men’s tennis (9-3, 3-0 Big Ten) will have the chance to do just that as it prepares to face Wisconsin (11-3, 2-2) on Friday and Nebraska (9-6, 1-3) on Sunday at the Los Angeles Tennis Center.
item.source:markdown: Rivalries bring intensity. But once the matchups are in the rearview, teams look to carry that energy forward. No. 24 UCLA men’s tennis (9-3, 3-0 Big Ten) will have the chance to do just that as it prepares to face Wisconsin (11-3, 2-2) on Friday and Nebraska (9-6, 1-3) on Sunday at the Los Angeles Tennis Center.

item.title: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Grok are all bad at crediting news outlets, but ChatGPT is the worst (at least in this study)
item.description: Canadian researchers asked the paid and free or “economy” versions of four AI models — ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Grok — about Canadian news events to see whether they would credit individual news outlets in their answers. The answer will probably not surprise you: AI models rarely cite news sources unless they’re specifically asked to,...
item.source:markdown: Canadian researchers asked the paid and free or “economy” versions of four AI models — ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Grok — about Canadian news events to see whether they would credit individual news outlets in their answers. item.title: UC approves $298.2 million budget to convert LA building into UCLA Health complex
item.description: The UC Board of Regents approved a $298.2 million budget Wednesday to convert a Los Angeles building into a UCLA Health complex. The project will convert an office building in El Segundo into UCLA Health South Bay, which will house a Sports Medicine Institute and create community fill and specialty pharmacies.
item.source:markdown: The UC Board of Regents approved a $298.2 million budget Wednesday to convert a Los Angeles building into a UCLA Health complex. The project will convert an office building in El Segundo into UCLA Health South Bay, which will house a Sports Medicine Institute and create community fill and specialty pharmacies.

item.title: Bruin baseball holds on, wins against the Waves 5-4 at Jackie Robinson Stadium
item.description: The song played in ballparks across the country may be failing to capture a key part of baseball. “Take Me Out to the Ballgame” ends with, “For it’s one, two, three strikes you’re out, / At the old ballgame.” But batters typically get more than a single plate appearance.
item.source:markdown: The song played in ballparks across the country may be failing to capture a key part of baseball. “Take Me Out to the Ballgame” ends with, “For it’s one, two, three strikes you’re out, / At the old ballgame.” But batters typically get more than a single plate appearance.

item.title: Oscars 2026: ‘One Battle After Another’ wins 6 awards, Michael B. Jordan claims Best Actor
item.description: The Academy’s 98th award show featured steep competition and Hollywood glamour Sunday night. Hitting the ground running, host Conan O’Brien opened cinema’s biggest night at the Dolby Theatre with a thrilling montage and monologue highlighting this year’s nominees, including meme-ifiable Leonardo DiCaprio of “One Battle After Another” and the newly-instated category for Casting.
item.source:markdown: The Academy’s 98th award show featured steep competition and Hollywood glamour Sunday night. Hitting the ground running, host Conan O’Brien opened cinema’s biggest night at the Dolby Theatre with a thrilling montage and monologue highlighting this year’s nominees, including meme-ifiable Leonardo DiCaprio of “One Battle After Another” and the newly-instated category for Casting.

item.title: Kiran Desai expands on new book’s theme of loneliness at Hammer Museum talk
item.description: Kiran Desai explored loneliness and connection across space and time at the Hammer Museum on Thursday. Desai – the Booker Prize and National Book Critics Circle Award-winning author of “Hullabaloo in the Guava Orchard” and “The Inheritance of Loss” – spoke with UCLA professor Mona Simpson in this year’s final iteration of Simpson’s “Some Favorite Writers” series.
item.source:markdown: Kiran Desai explored loneliness and connection across space and time at the Hammer Museum on Thursday. Desai – the Booker Prize and National Book Critics Circle Award-winning author of “Hullabaloo in the Guava Orchard” and “The Inheritance of Loss” – spoke with UCLA professor Mona Simpson in this year’s final iteration of Simpson’s “Some Favorite Writers” series.

item.title: UC reaches tentative agreements with 3 United Auto Workers units
item.description: The UC reached tentative agreements with three United Auto Workers units that had threatened to strike, the units announced Friday. The tentative agreement, if approved, will become the first contracts for Student Services and Advising Professionals-UAW and Research and Public Service Professionals-UAW, which were officially recognized by the University in April and September, respectively.
item.source:markdown: The UC reached tentative agreements with three United Auto Workers units that had threatened to strike, the units announced Friday. The tentative agreement, if approved, will become the first contracts for Student Services and Advising Professionals-UAW and Research and Public Service Professionals-UAW, which were officially recognized by the University in April and September, respectively.

item.description: Law professor Alex Wang credited China for its advancements in green technology but criticized its shortcomings in ecological protection at a book talk Wednesday. The talk was divided between Wang’s explanation of his book titled “Chinese Global Environmentalism” and a conversation between him and Mary Nichols, the former chair of the California Air Resources Board.

item.title: Students debate activist Chloe Cole at Turning Point USA at UCLA event
item.description: UCLA students debated a conservative activist who opposes gender-affirming care for minors at a Turning Point USA event in Bruin Plaza on Wednesday. item.title: UCLA men’s tennis beats San Diego in highest-ranked victory of the season
item.description: Rudy Quan had caused the earthquake. And the landslide was headed straight for him. Wielding open water bottles and shouting “Let’s go, Rudy,” the Bruins tackled the sophomore in celebration.

item.title: Scouting report: UCLA women’s basketball vs. USC
item.description: No. 2 UCLA women’s basketball (27-1, 17-0 Big Ten) will face USC (17-11, 9-8) on Sunday to conclude the regular season. The Bruins are entering the matchup at Galen Center on a 21-game win streak and with an undefeated record in conference play.
item.source:markdown: No. 2 UCLA women’s basketball (27-1, 17-0 Big Ten) will face USC (17-11, 9-8) on Sunday to conclude the regular season. The Bruins are entering the matchup at Galen Center on a 21-game win streak and with an undefeated record in conference play.

item.title: Wins at Judi Garman Classic make Kelly Inouye-Perez winningest UCLA softball coach
item.description: Historic feats breed legends. No. 9 UCLA softball (15-3) defeated East Texas A&M (5-15) 14-0 and No. 6 Florida (22-1) 15-12 in the first leg of the Judi Garman Classic in Fullerton, California, on Friday, marking coach Kelly Inouye-Perez’s 888th win as head coach.
item.source:markdown: Historic feats breed legends. No. 9 UCLA softball (15-3) defeated East Texas A&M (5-15) 14-0 and No. 6 Florida (22-1) 15-12 in the first leg of the Judi Garman Classic in Fullerton, California, on Friday, marking coach Kelly Inouye-Perez’s 888th win as head coach.

item.title: Scouting report: UCLA men’s basketball vs. Minnesota
item.description: Riding a two-game win streak – including an upset over No. 10 Illinois and a blowout of its crosstown rivals, UCLA men’s basketball (19-9, 11-6 Big Ten) will turn its head to Saturday morning, where it will face Minnesota (13-15, 6-11) at Pauley Pavilion for the penultimate road game of the regular season.
item.source:markdown: Riding a two-game win streak – including an upset over No. 10 Illinois and a blowout of its crosstown rivals, UCLA men’s basketball (19-9, 11-6 Big Ten) will turn its head to Saturday morning, where it will face Minnesota (13-15, 6-11) at Pauley Pavilion for the penultimate road game of the regular season.

item.title: UCLA gymnastics secures Big Ten regular season championship at Big Fours quad meet
item.description: This post was updated Feb. 27 at 10:07 p.m. The Bruins are the 2026 regular season Big Ten Champions.  After joining the Big Ten in the 2025, UCLA has yet to suffer a conference defeat in its first two regular seasons.  Tonight was no exception.
item.source:markdown: _This post was updated Feb. 27 at 10:07 p.m._ The Bruins are the 2026 regular season Big Ten Champions. After joining the Big Ten in the 2025, UCLA has yet to suffer a conference defeat in its first two regular seasons. Tonight was no exception.

item.title: Senate Bill 607 aims to authorize AI as research concentration at Cal ISIs
item.description: A state senator introduced a bill last month to formally recognize artificial intelligence as a research concentration at UC research institutes. If passed, Senate Bill 607 would allow one of the four California Institutes for Science and Innovation – which are multidisciplinary research institutes on UC campuses – to add AI as a concentration.
item.source:markdown: A state senator introduced a bill last month to formally recognize artificial intelligence as a research concentration at UC research institutes. If passed, Senate Bill 607 would allow one of the four California Institutes for Science and Innovation – which are multidisciplinary research institutes on UC campuses – to add AI as a concentration.

item.title: ‘We have a right to know what’s going on’: Sproul residents allege service issues
item.description: Wilson Her said his heart dropped when he saw both of Sproul Hall’s elevators were broken after returning from a trip in early February. He had 150 pounds of luggage and seven flights of stairs to climb in over 80 degree heat.
item.source:markdown: Wilson Her said his heart dropped when he saw both of Sproul Hall’s elevators were broken after returning from a trip in early February. He had 150 pounds of luggage and seven flights of stairs to climb in over 80 degree heat.

item.title: Fee-increasing referendums pass USAC, await chancellor approval for May ballots
item.description: UCLA students may be able to vote on one of the Undergraduate Students Association Council’s costliest referendums this May. USAC approved three referendums to appear on undergraduate students’ ballots in the May election – including the Bruin Success Referendum, Bruin Bash Referendum and Bruin Life and Undergraduate Experience Fee Referendum – at its Feb.

item.title: UCLA Police Department budget breakdown reflects difference in weaponry
item.description: The UCLA Police Department has the smallest stock of lethal weapons in the UC, but the largest stock of less-than-lethal munitions, according to data obtained from the university.
